required: Are there practice problems? (you need 3-5 per lecture for real learning)

effortModel.citation: r/PhysicsStudents DP-14: 2.0× multiplier confirmed; derivation-heavy → up to 3.0× (DP-15)

expectedStrategy: 1 lecture per day (NOT 2) — 100min lecture + 20min immediate problem practice
interleaved: each day = current lecture + review of previous lecture problems
Day 9-14: full review + past exam practice
warnings: watching alone ≠ studying — must pause every 10min and work out derivations
derivation-heavy lectures may take up to 150min (3.0×) — plan accordingly
alternatives: if lecture content is mostly review: 1.5× multiplier allows slight speedup
interleaved structure more effective than massed lecture binging
hardFailChecks: must NOT plan 2 lectures/day — physically impossible given budget
must include immediate problem-solving track alongside each lecture
ICAP principle: constructive (writing derivations) > passive watching
toneExpectation: constructive and structured; validate 'behind' feeling briefly then pivot to 'here's the achievable plan'; milestone-based progress
